asset_type: TaraHome technical blog cover, wide landscape
prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: TaraHome technical blog cover, wide landscape 3:2 composition
Primary request: a clean editorial residential photograph about simplifying the local network for Home Assistant, Matter, and Thread instead of splitting the core smart-home path across separate IoT VLANs
Scene/backdrop: a warm lived-in home's shallow built-in oak utility cabinet near an entry hallway, with pale plaster walls, woven linen texture, and a restrained dark-green ceramic catchall as the only decorative accent; a normal living space and softly glowing table lamp remain visible beyond the open cabinet
Subject: an organized shelf holding a compact unbranded white home Wi-Fi router, a small unbranded dark-green local smart-home mini hub, and a simple five-port Ethernet switch; one homeowner's hand naturally connects a short dark-green Ethernet cable between the switch and local hub; a small unbranded white door sensor is mounted normally on the nearby wood doorframe
Style/medium: photorealistic high-end editorial home photography, practical and believable, natural wood grain and cable texture, ordinary consumer networking equipment rather than a data-center rack or product render
Composition/framing: wide eye-level three-quarter framing, the hand and three local-network devices form one clear central focal group that remains recognizable in both 16:10 article crop and 16:9 card crop; tidy negative space at the edges; no split-screen composition
Lighting/mood: soft natural morning window light, warm neutral color balance, calm, secure, and useful rather than futuristic
Constraints: physically plausible Ethernet ports and cable placement; exactly one visible hand; devices rest naturally on the shelf; no brand logos; no readable text; no headline or poster; no watermark; no screen interface; no exaggerated LEDs
Avoid: decorative routing or network path lines, signal-wave overlays, charts, graphs, diagrams, translucent panels, inset frames, floating UI cards, progress bars, fake app screenshots, generic AI-looking smart-home diagrams, beige device lineup on a blank background, surreal hardware, glossy sci-fi styling, server racks, excessive cable clutter, duplicated routers or hands
